interpretation: FAM217B is a protein-coding gene that has been associated with various diseases, including neurodegenerative disorders and rheumatoid arthritis. However, there is no direct evidence linking FAM217B to Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH) from the retrieved sources. The gene's involvement in inflammatory processes, as seen in rheumatoid arthritis, may suggest a potential indirect role in bone health and osteonecrosis, but this connection remains speculative. Therefore, the evidence for FAM217B as a biomarker for SONFH is weak, relying primarily on inference rather than strong biological or clinical data. As such, its potential as an early-detection biomarker for SONFH is limited.
relevance_summary: FAM217B has no established connection to Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH) based on current evidence.

Description: A disorder of the central nervous system characterized by gradual and progressive loss of neural tissue and neurologic function.

result.title: FAM217B ↔ rheumatoid arthritis
result.text:
Disease: rheumatoid arthritis
Description: A chronic, systemic autoimmune disorder characterized by inflammation in the synovial membranes and articular surfaces. It manifests primarily as a symmetric, erosive polyarthritis that spares the axial skeleton and is typically associated with the presence in the serum of rheumatoid factor.
Association score: 0.121
